use_cases.title: Beaconing — periodic outbound to small set of destinations
use_cases.description: C2 channel detection via inter-beacon-time stddev / fan-out to single dest.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count, values(All_Traffic.dest_port) AS ports
    from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
    where All_Traffic.action="allowed" AND All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal"
    by _time span=10s, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
| streamstats current=f last(_time) AS prev_time by src, dest
| eval delta = _time - prev_time
| stats avg(delta) AS avg_delta stdev(delta) AS sd_delta count by src, dest
| where count > 30 AND sd_delta < 5 AND avg_delta>=30 AND avg_delta<=600
| sort - count
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public" and ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| project DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, Timestamp
| sort by DeviceName asc, RemoteIP asc, RemotePort asc, Timestamp asc
| extend prev_dev = prev(DeviceName, 1), prev_ip = prev(RemoteIP, 1),
         prev_port = prev(RemotePort, 1), prev_ts = prev(Timestamp, 1)
| where DeviceName == prev_dev and RemoteIP == prev_ip and RemotePort == prev_port
| extend delta_sec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, prev_ts)
| summarize conn_count = count(), avg_delta = avg(delta_sec), stdev_delta = stdev(delta_sec)
    by DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| where conn_count > 30 and avg_delta between (30.0 .. 600.0) and stdev_delta < 5.0
| order by conn_count desc

use_cases.title: Suspicious browser extension installation
use_cases.description: Side-loaded / unsigned extensions, often masquerading as wallets, productivity tools.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry
    where (Registry.registry_path="*\Software\Google\Chrome\Extensions\*"
        OR Registry.registry_path="*\Software\Microsoft\Edge\Extensions\*"
        OR Registry.registry_path="*\Software\Mozilla\Firefox\Extensions\*")
    by Registry.dest, Registry.registry_path, Registry.registry_value_data, Registry.registry_value_name, Registry.user
| `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where RegistryKey has_any ("\Software\Google\Chrome\Extensions\","\Software\Microsoft\Edge\Extensions\","\Software\Mozilla\Firefox\Extensions\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName

use_cases.title: Infostealer — non-browser process accessing browser cookie/login DBs
use_cases.description: Stealers (RedLine, Lumma, Vidar, Atomic) read Login Data / cookies SQLite from Chrome/Edge/Firefox.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem
    where (Filesystem.file_path="*\Google\Chrome\User Data\*\Login Data*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Google\Chrome\User Data\*\Cookies*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\*\Login Data*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\*\logins.json*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\*\cookies.sqlite*")
      AND NOT Filesystem.process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe")
    by Filesystem.dest, Filesystem.process_name, Filesystem.file_path, Filesystem.user
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\", @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\")
| where FileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","logins.json","cookies.sqlite")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FolderPath, FileName, ActionType

use_cases.title: Crypto-wallet file/keystore access by non-wallet process
use_cases.description: Stealers / wallet drainers read keystore.json, MetaMask, Exodus, Atomic, Phantom data.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem
    where (Filesystem.file_path="*\Ethereum\keystore\*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Bitcoin\wallet.dat"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Exodus\exodus.wallet*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Electrum\wallets\*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\MetaMask\*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Phantom\*"
        OR Filesystem.file_path="*\Atomic\Local Storage\*")
      AND NOT Filesystem.process_name IN ("MetaMask.exe","Exodus.exe","Atomic.exe","electrum.exe","Bitcoin.exe","Phantom.exe")
    by Filesystem.dest, Filesystem.process_name, Filesystem.file_path, Filesystem.user
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Ethereum\keystore\", @"\Bitcoin\", @"\Exodus\", @"\Electrum\wallets\", @"\MetaMask\", @"\Phantom\", @"\Atomic\Local Storage\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("MetaMask.exe","Exodus.exe","Atomic.exe","electrum.exe","Bitcoin.exe","Phantom.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FolderPath, FileName, ActionType

use_cases.title: 1Password failed sign-in burst
use_cases.description: Failed 1Password logins — credential spray indicator. Datadog default 'Anomalous amount of failed sign-in attempts by 1Password user'.

use_cases.title: 1Password impossible-travel sign-in
use_cases.description: Geographically impossible 1Password sign-in. Datadog default 'Impossible travel event observed from 1Password user'.

use_cases.title: 1Password item exfiltration attempt
use_cases.description: Suspicious item-level access — credential-theft pattern. Datadog default 'Attempt to exfiltrate a 1Password item by user'.

use_cases.title: 1Password activity from Tor exit node
use_cases.description: 1Password access from Tor — credential-vault foreign-source flag. Datadog default '1Password activity observed from Tor client IP'.

use_cases.title: 1Password vault export attempted
use_cases.description: Vault export — bulk credential exfil. Datadog default '1Password vault export attempt by user'.

use_cases.title: Abnormal Security: brute-force attack detected
use_cases.description: Abnormal Security flagged brute-force activity. Datadog default 'Abnormal Security Brute Force Attack'.

use_cases.title: Abnormal Security: login from new location
use_cases.description: Login from a previously-unseen geo for the user. Datadog default 'Abnormal Security Login New Location'.

use_cases.title: Abnormal Security: malicious email opened
use_cases.description: User opened a malicious email Abnormal flagged. Datadog default 'Abnormal Security Malicious Email Opened'.

use_cases.title: Authentication not detected on admin API endpoint
use_cases.description: Admin API route serving 2xx responses without an Authorization header or session cookie — exposed admin surface. Datadog default 'Authentication not detected on admin endpoint'.

use_cases.title: Unauthenticated route returns sensitive PII
use_cases.description: API endpoint without authentication returning PII (email, phone, SSN-shape, card-shape) in its response body — direct data-exposure path. Datadog default 'Unauthenticated route returns sensitive PII'.

use_cases.title: Excessive resource consumption of third-party API
use_cases.description: Abnormal request volume to a third-party API endpoint — resource abuse, scraping, or DoS-staging behaviour. Datadog default 'Excessive resource consumption of third-party API'.

use_cases.title: JWT authentication bypass attempt
use_cases.description: JWT manipulation / none-alg / kid-abuse signatures. Datadog default 'JWT authentication bypass attempt'.

use_cases.title: Local File Inclusion (LFI) exploited
use_cases.description: LFI WAF rule fired with 2xx response — successful file read. Datadog default 'Local file inclusion exploited'.

use_cases.title: Spring4Shell RCE attempts (CVE-2022-22963)
use_cases.description: Spring4Shell exploitation attempts. Datadog default 'Spring4shell RCE attempts - CVE-2022-22963'.

use_cases.title: Application user activity from Tor
use_cases.description: App-side activity from Tor exit — anonymisation flag. Datadog default 'User activity from Tor'.

use_cases.title: Atlassian user added to administrative group
use_cases.description: User added to an Atlassian admin group. Datadog default 'Atlassian user added to administrative group'.

use_cases.title: Atlassian administrator impersonating user
use_cases.description: Admin took over a user session — investigate operational legitimacy. Datadog default 'Atlassian administrator impersonated user'.

use_cases.title: Auth0 anomalous attack-protection event spike
use_cases.description: Spike in Auth0 attack-protection trigger events. Datadog default 'Auth0 Anomalous Attack Protection Events'.

use_cases.title: Auth0 breached-password detection disabled
use_cases.description: Breached-password detection turned off — adversary lowering the auth bar. Datadog default 'Auth0 Breached Password Detection Disabled'.

use_cases.title: Auth0 login with known-breached password
use_cases.description: User authenticated with a credential known from a breach corpus. Datadog default 'Auth0 Breached Password Login'.

use_cases.title: Auth0 brute-force attack on user
use_cases.description: Auth0's per-user brute-force lockout fired. Datadog default 'Brute Force Attack on Auth0 User'.

use_cases.title: Auth0 brute-force protection disabled
use_cases.description: Auth0 brute-force protection disabled. Datadog default 'Auth0 Brute Force Protection Disabled'.

use_cases.title: Auth0 credential-stuffing attack
use_cases.description: Auth0's distributed-credential-stuffing detector fired. Datadog default 'Credential Stuffing Attack on Auth0'.

use_cases.title: Auth0 impossible-travel sign-in
use_cases.description: Auth0 successful login from geographically impossible locations. Datadog default 'Impossible Travel Auth0 Login'.

use_cases.title: Auth0 suspicious-IP throttling disabled
use_cases.description: Auth0 suspicious-IP throttling turned off. Datadog default 'Auth0 Suspicious IP Throttling Disabled'.

use_cases.title: AWS access key created (programmatic credential)
use_cases.description: New IAM access key created — long-lived credential issued, watch for abuse. Datadog default 'AWS access key creation by unusual principal'.

use_cases.title: AWS brute-force ConsoleLogin then AssumeRole
use_cases.description: Failed ConsoleLogin attempts against the same identity correlated with a successful AssumeRole call shortly after — credential-spray landing on a privileged role. Datadog default 'Brute forced ConsoleLogin event correlates with assumed role'.

use_cases.title: AWS CloudTrail logging configuration modified
use_cases.description: CloudTrail logging stopped, trail deleted, or selectors changed — blinding the audit pipeline. Datadog default 'AWS CloudTrail configuration modified'.

use_cases.title: AWS Config service modified or stopped
use_cases.description: AWS Config recorder stopped or deleted — compliance trail blinded. Datadog default 'AWS Config modified'.

use_cases.title: AWS Console login without MFA + impossible travel
use_cases.description: AWS ConsoleLogin events for an identity that didn't use MFA, where the same principal also signed in from a geographically distant location within a tight window (impossible travel). Mirrors Datadog's default rule 'AWS ConsoleLogin without MFA triggered Impossible Travel scenario'.

use_cases.title: AWS EC2 security group rules modified
use_cases.description: EC2 security-group rules changed — adversary opening a path or covering tracks. Datadog default 'AWS EC2 security group modified'.

use_cases.title: AWS ECS cluster deleted
use_cases.description: ECS cluster removed — workload impact. Datadog default 'AWS ECS cluster deleted'.

use_cases.title: AWS GuardDuty detector disabled or deleted
use_cases.description: GuardDuty turned off — defenders' main AWS-native threat detection silenced. Datadog default 'AWS GuardDuty detector disabled'.

use_cases.title: AWS IAM AdministratorAccess policy applied to a user
use_cases.description: An IAM user / group / role just got AdministratorAccess — direct admin grant rather than going through the org's standard provisioning path. Datadog default 'AWS IAM AdministratorAccess policy was applied to a user'.

use_cases.title: Impossible travel observed for IAM user
use_cases.description: IAM user activity from geographically distant locations within an impossible-travel window. Datadog default 'Impossible travel observed on IAM user'.

use_cases.title: AWS IAM policy created / updated / version changed
use_cases.description: IAM policy added or version changed — privilege drift. Datadog default 'AWS IAM policy modified'.

use_cases.title: AWS KMS key deleted or scheduled for deletion
use_cases.description: KMS key scheduled for deletion or deleted outright — disables decryption of everything the key wrapped (S3, EBS, RDS). Ransomware / insider-impact pattern. Datadog default 'AWS KMS key deleted or scheduled for deletion'.

use_cases.title: AWS Lambda function code or configuration modified
use_cases.description: Lambda function code/config changed — adversary persistence via serverless backdoor. Datadog default 'AWS Lambda function modified'.

use_cases.title: AWS RDS DB cluster deleted
use_cases.description: RDS DB cluster or instance deleted — destructive impact. Datadog default 'AWS RDS cluster deleted'.

use_cases.title: AWS root account activity (any action)
use_cases.description: Any console / API action by the AWS root identity. Steady-state should be ZERO; every hit is an investigation. Datadog default 'AWS root account activity'.

use_cases.title: AWS S3 bucket policy modified
use_cases.description: S3 bucket access policy changed — pivot to find adversary granting themselves read or write. Datadog default 'AWS S3 bucket policy modified'.

use_cases.title: AWS S3 bucket ACL / policy made public
use_cases.description: S3 bucket ACL or policy modified to allow access from AllUsers / AllAuthenticatedUsers, or PublicAccessBlock removed. Either misconfig or intentional data-exposure prep. Datadog default 'AWS S3 Bucket ACL made public'.

use_cases.title: AWS SecurityHub disabled
use_cases.description: SecurityHub turned off — aggregated AWS findings silenced. Datadog default 'AWS SecurityHub disabled'.

use_cases.title: AWS VPC Flow Log deleted
use_cases.description: VPC Flow Logs deleted — adversary erasing network-traffic visibility. Datadog default 'AWS VPC Flow Log deleted'.

use_cases.title: Azure AD brute-force login
use_cases.description: Multiple failed Azure AD sign-ins against the same UPN within a short window — credential spray / brute force. Datadog default 'Azure AD brute force login'.

use_cases.title: Azure AD member assigned Global Administrator role
use_cases.description: A user just got the Azure AD Global Administrator role — top-of-tenant privilege. Datadog default 'Azure AD member assigned Global Administrator role'.

use_cases.title: Azure AD MFA disabled for a user
use_cases.description: MFA was turned off for a user — adversary post-compromise persistence step (remove the second factor before re-using the credential). Datadog default 'Azure AD MFA disabled'.

use_cases.title: Azure diagnostic setting deleted
use_cases.description: Azure diagnostic-setting deletion blinds activity-log forwarding to SIEM. Datadog default 'Azure diagnostic setting deleted'.

use_cases.title: Azure Key Vault keys / secrets read
use_cases.description: Key Vault secret/key reads — pivot for credential-theft tradecraft. Datadog default 'Azure Key Vault access keys viewed'.

use_cases.title: Azure new owner added to service principal
use_cases.description: Service-principal ownership change — adversary getting persistent control of an app identity. Datadog default 'Azure new owner added to service principal'.

use_cases.title: Azure SQL Server firewall rule created
use_cases.description: Azure SQL firewall rule added — opening DB to a new IP range. Datadog default 'Azure SQL Server Firewall rule created'.

use_cases.title: Azure storage soft-delete disabled
use_cases.description: Soft-delete turned off — adversary preparing to destroy blobs without recovery. Datadog default 'Azure storage account soft delete disabled'.

use_cases.title: Azure user added to administrative group
use_cases.description: User joined an Azure admin group — privilege escalation path. Datadog default 'Azure user added to administrative group'.

use_cases.title: Command injection exploited (WAF detection)
use_cases.description: WAF telemetry showing successful command-injection against a web application — shell metacharacters in a request that returned 2xx. Datadog default 'Command injection exploited'.

use_cases.title: Confluence global security setting changed
use_cases.description: Global Confluence security setting modified. Datadog default 'Confluence Global Setting Changed'.

use_cases.title: Confluence page public link created
use_cases.description: Public-link sharing of an internal page. Datadog default 'Confluence Public Link Creation'.

use_cases.title: Confluence space export
use_cases.description: Space-level export — bulk content exfil. Datadog default 'Confluence Space Export'.

use_cases.title: Container escape attempt detected
use_cases.description: Process tried to break out of container isolation — direct host-compromise primitive. Datadog default 'Container escape attempt detected'.

use_cases.title: Credential-stuffing attack on application

use_cases.description: Forwarded CrowdStrike sensor detection — pivot to the original CS console. Datadog default 'CrowdStrike Alert Detection'.

use_cases.description: Bulk repo deletion in a tight window — destructive impact. Datadog default 'GitHub Mass Repository Deletion'.

use_cases.description: Org-wide 2FA requirement removed — auth bar lowered. Datadog default 'GitHub MFA requirement disabled'.

use_cases.description: Org dropped out of enterprise scope — admin oversight loss. Datadog default 'GitHub organization removed from enterprise'.

use_cases.description: New PAT issued — long-lived credential watch-list. Datadog default 'GitHub personal access token created'.

use_cases.description: Same PAT seen from geographically distant locations within an impossible-travel window. Datadog default 'GitHub PAT Impossible Travel'.

use_cases.description: PAT used to clone an unusual number of repositories — credential theft + source-code exfil. Datadog default 'GitHub personal access token used to clone repositories'.

use_cases.description: Private repo flipped to public — unintentional or intentional source-code exposure. Datadog default 'GitHub repository visibility changed to public'.

use_cases.description: Repo transferred to another owner — IP-leak path. Datadog default 'GitHub repository transfer initiated'.

use_cases.description: User gained admin on GitLab tenant. Datadog default 'GitLab administrator added'.

use_cases.description: Private GitLab group flipped to public — exposure. Datadog default 'GitLab group visibility changed to public'.

use_cases.description: Two-step verification turned off for an organisational unit. Datadog default 'Google Workspace Admin 2SV Disabled'.

use_cases.description: Auto-forward to a non-corporate domain — classic mail exfil. Datadog default 'Google Workspace Email Forwarding External'.

use_cases.title: Google Workspace admin role assigned to user
use_cases.description: User granted Workspace admin privileges. Datadog default 'Google Workspace User Admin Role Assignment'.

use_cases.title: Impossible travel from application business-logic event
use_cases.description: Successful authentication for the same user from geographically distant locations within a window that's physically impossible to traverse. Datadog default 'Impossible travel observed from business logic event'.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes ClusterRole / binding deleted
use_cases.description: ClusterRole / binding removed — defenders losing access or adversary covering tracks. Datadog default 'Kubernetes ClusterRole binding deleted'.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes pod created with privileged flag
use_cases.description: Privileged pod — direct host-takeover primitive. Datadog default 'Kubernetes pod executed with privileged flag'.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes RBAC role binding created
use_cases.description: RBAC binding added — privilege drift inside the cluster. Datadog default 'Kubernetes RBAC role binding created'.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes Secret accessed
use_cases.description: Secret read by a non-system principal — credential-theft signal. Datadog default 'Kubernetes secret accessed'.

use_cases.title: Kubernetes admission webhook configuration modified
use_cases.description: Admission webhook changed — adversary inserting cluster-wide interception. Datadog default 'Kubernetes webhook configuration modified'.

use_cases.title: Log4Shell RCE attempts (CVE-2021-44228)
use_cases.description: JNDI-lookup payloads (${jndi:ldap://...} / ${jndi:rmi://...}) in HTTP headers or request bodies — Log4Shell exploitation attempts. Datadog default 'Log4shell RCE attempts (CVE-2021-44228)'.

use_cases.title: M365 admin role assigned to user
use_cases.description: User became an M365 admin — privilege drift. Datadog default 'M365 admin role assigned'.

use_cases.title: M365 mail-forwarding rule created
use_cases.description: Auto-forward rule — classic adversary exfil persistence. Datadog default 'M365 forwarding rule created'.

use_cases.title: M365 mailbox delegation granted
use_cases.description: Inbox delegation — adversary inbox-rule pre-staging. Datadog default 'M365 mailbox delegation granted'.

use_cases.title: M365 MFA disabled for a user
use_cases.description: MFA removed — adversary auth-bar lowering. Datadog default 'M365 MFA disabled'.

use_cases.title: MongoDB authentication disabled
use_cases.description: MongoDB auth turned off — anyone can connect. Datadog default 'MongoDB authentication disabled'.

use_cases.title: MongoDB database dropped
use_cases.description: DB dropped — destructive event. Datadog default 'MongoDB database deleted'.

use_cases.title: MongoDB user created
use_cases.description: New DB user — pivot for legitimacy review. Datadog default 'MongoDB user created'.

use_cases.title: MongoDB user role escalated
use_cases.description: DB user got a higher role — privilege drift. Datadog default 'MongoDB user privilege escalated'.

use_cases.title: Okta administrative role assigned to user
use_cases.description: User gets an Okta admin role — privilege drift. Datadog default 'Okta administrative role assigned'.

use_cases.title: Okta application access granted to user
use_cases.description: User added to an Okta-mediated app — pivot for entitlement audits. Datadog default 'Okta application access granted'.

use_cases.title: Okta authentication / sign-on policy modified
use_cases.description: Auth policy / rule changed — adversary lowering the auth bar. Datadog default 'Okta authentication policy modified'.

use_cases.title: Okta MFA bypass attempt
use_cases.description: MFA challenge failed in a way that suggests a bypass attempt. Datadog default 'Okta MFA bypass attempt'.

use_cases.title: Okta user account locked
use_cases.description: Okta user lockout — credential-spray indicator (and a paste-target for IP / target lists). Datadog default 'Okta user account locked'.

use_cases.title: PostgreSQL authentication method modified
use_cases.description: pg_hba reload or auth setting change — bar lowering. Datadog default 'PostgreSQL authentication method modified'.

use_cases.title: PostgreSQL database dropped
use_cases.description: DB dropped — destructive event. Datadog default 'PostgreSQL database deleted'.

use_cases.title: PostgreSQL superuser role created
use_cases.description: Superuser role created — DBA-level privilege drift. Datadog default 'PostgreSQL role created with superuser'.

use_cases.title: Snowflake role created
use_cases.description: New Snowflake role defined — privilege drift in the warehouse. Datadog default 'Snowflake role created'.

use_cases.title: Snowflake share created or modified
use_cases.description: Cross-account data share opened — direct data-exfil primitive. Datadog default 'Snowflake share created or modified'.

use_cases.title: Snowflake user added to role
use_cases.description: User got new role — entitlement audit. Datadog default 'Snowflake user added to role'.

use_cases.title: SQL injection exploited (WAF detection)
use_cases.description: WAF telemetry showing successful SQL injection against a web application — request payload matched SQLi signatures and returned a 2xx instead of being blocked. Datadog default 'SQL injection exploited'.

use_cases.title: SSRF exploited (WAF detection)
use_cases.description: WAF telemetry showing successful SSRF — request includes link-local / metadata IP or internal RFC1918 address as the target, application returned 2xx. Datadog default 'SSRF exploited'.

use_cases.title: DNS tunneling / TXT-heavy domain queries
use_cases.description: Long subdomain labels + frequent queries to a single 2LD = DNS C2/exfil.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS
    where DNS.message_type="QUERY"
    by DNS.src, DNS.query
| `drop_dm_object_name(DNS)`
| eval qlen=len(query)
| where qlen > 50
| rex field=query "(?<second_level_domain>[\w-]+\.[\w-]+)$"
| stats sum(count) AS qcount, dc(query) AS unique_subs, max(qlen) AS max_label
    by src, second_level_domain
| where qcount > 100 AND unique_subs > 20
| sort - qcount
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where RemotePort == 53 and isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| extend qlen = strlen(RemoteUrl)
| where qlen > 50
| extend SecondLevelDomain = extract(@"([\w-]+\.[a-zA-Z]{2,})$", 1, RemoteUrl)
| summarize qcount = count(), uniqueSubs = dcount(RemoteUrl), maxLabel = max(qlen)
    by DeviceName, SecondLevelDomain
| where qcount > 100 and uniqueSubs > 20
| order by qcount desc

use_cases.title: Fake CAPTCHA / clipboard-injected PowerShell (ClickFix / FakeCaptcha)
use_cases.description: Browser-pasted PowerShell launched from explorer/Run dialog — ClickFix social engineering.

mitre_attack.name: User Execution: Malicious Copy and Paste

use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","RuntimeBroker.exe")
      AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe")
      AND (Processes.process="*iex*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-Expression*"
        OR Processes.process="*FromBase64*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*"
        OR Processes.process="*hxxp*" OR Processes.process="*curl*" OR Processes.process="*wget*")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process, Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","RuntimeBroker.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(iex|invoke-expression|frombase64|downloadstring|hxxp|curl |wget )"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine

use_cases.title: File hash IOCs — endpoint file/process match
use_cases.description: Match SHA256/SHA1/MD5 named in the article against EDR file/process telemetry.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem
    where Filesystem.file_hash IN (__HASH_LIST__)
    by Filesystem.dest, Filesystem.user, Filesystem.file_path, Filesystem.file_name, Filesystem.file_hash
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
| append
    [| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
        where Processes.process_hash IN (__HASH_LIST__)
        by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process_name, Processes.process_hash
     | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`]
use_cases.defender_kql:
// Pre-filter each table BEFORE the union — the engine cannot push
// predicates across `union`, and DeviceFileEvents lacks ProcessCommandLine
// (Initiating only) so column-projection has to differ per side.
union
    ( DeviceFileEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
        | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
        | where SHA256 in~ (__HASH_LIST__) or SHA1 in~ (__HASH_LIST__) or MD5 in~ (__HASH_LIST__)
        | project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
                  Cmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, Source = "DeviceFileEvents" ),
    ( DeviceProcessEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
        | where AccountName !endswith "$"
        | where SHA256 in~ (__HASH_LIST__) or SHA1 in~ (__HASH_LIST__) or MD5 in~ (__HASH_LIST__)
        | project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
                  Cmd = ProcessCommandLine, Source = "DeviceProcessEvents" )
| order by Timestamp desc

use_cases.title: Remote service execution — PsExec / SMB lateral movement
use_cases.description: psexec / paexec / smbexec / wmic /node — service install over SMB from remote host.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.process_name IN ("psexec.exe","psexesvc.exe","paexec.exe","smbexec.py")
       OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*/node:*")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process_name, Processes.process, Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("psexec.exe","psexesvc.exe","paexec.exe","smbexec.py")
   or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/node:")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by Timestamp desc

use_cases.title: LSASS process access / dump (credential theft)
use_cases.description: Mimikatz, comsvcs.dll MiniDump, or any non-Windows process opening LSASS.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where (Processes.process="*lsass*" OR Processes.process="*sekurlsa*"
        OR Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*"
        OR Processes.process="*procdump*lsass*")
       OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND Processes.process="*comsvcs*MiniDump*")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process_name, Processes.process, Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where ActionType == "OpenProcessApiCall"
| where FileName =~ "lsass.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("MsSense.exe","MsMpEng.exe","csrss.exe",
                                          "svchost.exe","wininit.exe","services.exe",
                                          "lsm.exe","SearchProtocolHost.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FileName,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFolderPath, AccountName
| order by Timestamp desc

use_cases.title: MFA fatigue / push-bombing
use_cases.description: Many MFA pushes to same user in short window — suggests adversary attempting approval-fatigue.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
    where Authentication.action="failure" AND Authentication.signature="*MFA*"
    by _time span=5m, Authentication.user, Authentication.src
| `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)`
| where count > 10
use_cases.defender_kql:
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where ErrorCode in (50074, 50076, 50158, 50125, 50097)
| extend MfaPrompt = AuthenticationRequirement == "multiFactorAuthentication"
| where MfaPrompt
| summarize attempts = count(), distinct_ips = dcount(IPAddress)
    by AccountUpn, bin(Timestamp, 5m)
| where attempts > 10
| order by attempts desc

use_cases.title: Network connections to article IPs / domains
use_cases.description: Outbound traffic to attacker infrastructure named in the article.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
    where All_Traffic.dest IN (__IP_LIST__)
    by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
| append
    [| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Web
        where Web.dest IN (__DOMAIN_LIST__)
        by Web.src, Web.dest, Web.url, Web.user
     | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)`]
| append
    [| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS
        where DNS.query IN (__DOMAIN_LIST__)
        by DNS.src, DNS.query, DNS.answer
     | `drop_dm_object_name(DNS)`]
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteIP in (__IP_LIST__) or RemoteUrl has_any (__DOMAIN_LIST__)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine

use_cases.title: OAuth consent / suspicious app grant
use_cases.description: Cloud identity abuse: app gets high-priv scopes, often via consent phishing.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
    where Authentication.action="success"
      AND Authentication.signature IN (
        "Consent to application",
        "Add app role assignment grant to user",
        "Add OAuth2PermissionGrant",
        "Add delegated permission grant")
    by Authentication.user, Authentication.app, Authentication.src, Authentication.signature
| `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("Consent to application.","Add OAuth2PermissionGrant.","Add delegated permission grant.")
| project Timestamp, AccountObjectId, AccountDisplayName, ActivityType,
          ActivityObjects, IPAddress, UserAgent

use_cases.title: Office app spawning script/LOLBin child process
use_cases.description: Classic macro/exploit pattern — Office app launching cmd/powershell/wmic/regsvr32.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","onenote.exe","mspub.exe","visio.exe")
      AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.process_name, Processes.process
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","onenote.exe","mspub.exe","visio.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine

use_cases.title: Email attachment opened from external sender
use_cases.description: Attachment delivery + execution by recipient — most common malware initial access.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
    from datamodel=Email.All_Email
    where All_Email.file_name!="-"
    by All_Email.src_user, All_Email.recipient, All_Email.file_name, All_Email.subject
| rename All_Email.recipient as user
| join type=inner user
    [| tstats `summariesonly` count
        from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
        where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("OUTLOOK.EXE","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe")
          AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
        by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.process_name, Processes.process
     | rename Processes.user as user]
use_cases.defender_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let MalAttachments = EmailAttachmentInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project NetworkMessageId, RecipientEmailAddress,
              AttachmentFileName = FileName, AttachmentSHA256 = SHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("OUTLOOK.EXE","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
                      "mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| join kind=inner MalAttachments on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, AttachmentFileName, AttachmentSHA256

use_cases.title: Phishing-link click correlated to endpoint execution
use_cases.description:
User received an external email containing a URL, clicked it, and within
60 seconds the recipient's endpoint spawned a scripting interpreter or
network downloader (PowerShell / cmd / mshta / curl / certutil / bitsadmin).
This is the high-fidelity shape — a raw "user clicked an emailed URL"
query is too noisy to alert on because every Microsoft 365 tenant
generates thousands of legitimate clicks per day. The exec-correlation
step suppresses the noise to roughly the rate at which a click actually
drives onto-host execution, which is the actionable signal.
Pair with UC_PHISH_LINK_BYPASS (Safe Links override) and UC_PHISH_LINK_NEWDOMAIN
for orthogonal high-fidelity variants.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
``` Phishing-link click that drives endpoint execution within 60s ```
| tstats `summariesonly` earliest(_time) AS click_time
    from datamodel=Web
    where Web.action="allowed"
    by Web.src, Web.user, Web.dest, Web.url
| `drop_dm_object_name(Web)`
| rename user AS recipient, dest AS clicked_domain, url AS clicked_url
| join type=inner recipient
    [| tstats `summariesonly` count
         from datamodel=Email.All_Email
         where All_Email.action="delivered" AND All_Email.url!="-"
         by All_Email.recipient, All_Email.src_user, All_Email.url, All_Email.subject
     | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)`
     | rex field=url "https?://(?<email_domain>[^/]+)"
     | rename recipient AS recipient]
| join type=inner src
    [| tstats `summariesonly` earliest(_time) AS exec_time
         values(Processes.process) AS exec_cmd, values(Processes.process_name) AS exec_proc
         from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
         where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe",
                                                   "outlook.exe","brave.exe","arc.exe")
           AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe",
                                            "rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe",
                                            "cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe",
                                            "curl.exe","wget.exe")
         by Processes.dest, Processes.user
     | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
     | rename dest AS src]
| eval delta_sec = exec_time - click_time
| where delta_sec >= 0 AND delta_sec <= 60
| table click_time, exec_time, delta_sec, recipient, src, src_user, subject,
        clicked_domain, clicked_url, exec_proc, exec_cmd
| sort - click_time
use_cases.defender_kql:
// Phishing-link click that drives endpoint execution within 60s.
// Far higher fidelity than "every clicked URL" — most legitimate clicks
// never spawn a non-browser child process, so the join eliminates the
// 99% of noise that makes a raw click query unactionable.
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let SuspectClicks = UrlClickEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
        | where DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
        | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
        | project NetworkMessageId, Subject, SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain,
                  RecipientEmailAddress, EmailTimestamp = Timestamp
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | join kind=leftouter (
        EmailUrlInfo | project NetworkMessageId, Url, UrlDomain
      ) on NetworkMessageId, Url
    | project ClickTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Url, UrlDomain,
              Subject, SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain, RecipientEmailAddress,
              ActionType;
// Correlate to a non-browser child process spawned within 60 seconds on
// the recipient's device.
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe",
                                         "outlook.exe","brave.exe","arc.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe",
                        "rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
                        "bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe")
| join kind=inner SuspectClicks on $left.AccountName == $right.AccountUpn
| where Timestamp between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + 60s)
| project ClickTime, ProcessTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ClickTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress,
          Subject, Url, UrlDomain, ActionType,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by ClickTime desc

use_cases.title: User clicked through a Safe Links warning page
use_cases.description:
Microsoft Safe Links rendered a warning page for a URL and the user
clicked past it anyway (ActionType=ClickedThrough). This is an
explicit user-override of a defender control — by definition rare and
high-signal. Investigate every hit; the URL was flagged for a reason.
use_cases.defender_kql:
// Safe Links assessed the URL as risky, showed a warning page, and the
// user proceeded anyway. ClickedThrough events are inherently rare —
// every hit deserves analyst review. UrlDomain isn't on UrlClickEvents
// or EmailEvents — it's only on EmailUrlInfo, so we left-outer join it.
UrlClickEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType == "ClickedThrough"
| join kind=leftouter (
    EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | project NetworkMessageId, Subject, SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain,
              RecipientEmailAddress, EmailDirection
  ) on NetworkMessageId
| join kind=leftouter (
    EmailUrlInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | project NetworkMessageId, Url, UrlDomain
  ) on NetworkMessageId, Url
| project Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Url, UrlDomain,
          EmailDirection, SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain,
          RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, Workload
| order by Timestamp desc

use_cases.title: Click on URL whose host doesn't match the sender domain
use_cases.description:
User clicked an inbound-email URL where the URL host is unrelated to
the sender's authenticated domain. Most legitimate B2B / B2C senders
use links on their own domain, their CDN, or a small number of
marketing trackers. A click on a sender-mismatched URL — especially
to a cheap-TLD throwaway (.sbs, .top, .icu, .cyou) — is a moderate
phishing-class signal. Pair with UC_PHISH_LINK (exec correlation)
for the higher-confidence variant.
use_cases.defender_kql:
// Click on inbound-email URL where url-domain != sender-domain.
// The sender-domain root and url-domain root are extracted then
// compared; a mismatch on a cheap-TLD url-domain is the high-signal
// case. Tune the legit-marketer allowlist to your environment to
// suppress the residual newsletter / vendor-tracker noise.
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let LegitTrackers = dynamic(["mailchimp.com","sendgrid.net","sendgrid.com",
    "constantcontact.com","hubspotlinks.com","hubspot.com","mktoresp.com",
    "marketo.com","pardot.com","exacttarget.com","klaviyomail.com",
    "list-manage.com","createsend1.com","createsend.com","sg.actblue.com",
    "ml-attach.com","emltrk.com","amazonses.com","amazon.com","sndarsffrt.com",
    "salesforce.com","force.com","docusign.net","echosign.com",
    "office365.com","microsoft.com","onmicrosoft.com","linkedin.com",
    "github.com","githubusercontent.com","atlassian.net","jira.com"]);
let CheapTlds = dynamic(["sbs","life","top","icu","cyou","gdn","click","loan",
    "rest","monster","work","cn","ru","tk","ml","cf","ga"]);
EmailEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where DeliveryAction == "Delivered" and EmailDirection == "Inbound"
| extend SenderRoot = extract(@"([^.]+\.[a-zA-Z]+)$", 1, tolower(SenderFromDomain))
| join kind=inner (
    EmailUrlInfo | project NetworkMessageId, Url, UrlDomain
  ) on NetworkMessageId
| join kind=inner (
    UrlClickEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
    | project Url, ClickTimestamp = Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress
  ) on Url
| extend UrlDomLower = tolower(UrlDomain)
| extend UrlRoot = extract(@"([^.]+\.[a-zA-Z]+)$", 1, UrlDomLower)
| extend UrlTld = extract(@"\.([a-zA-Z]+)$", 1, UrlDomLower)
| where UrlRoot != SenderRoot
   and UrlRoot !in (LegitTrackers)
   and UrlDomLower !endswith ".microsoft.com"
   and UrlDomLower !endswith ".office.com"
   and UrlDomLower !endswith ".sharepoint.com"
| extend RiskScore = case(UrlTld in (CheapTlds), 3,
                          UrlRoot endswith ".ru" or UrlRoot endswith ".cn", 2,
                          1)
| where RiskScore >= 2
| project ClickTimestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, RecipientEmailAddress,
          SenderFromAddress, SenderRoot, UrlDomain, UrlRoot, UrlTld,
          RiskScore, Subject, Url
| order by RiskScore desc, ClickTimestamp desc

use_cases.title: PowerShell encoded / obfuscated command
use_cases.description: Encoded or obfuscated PowerShell — common across loaders, recon, and post-exploitation.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
      AND (Processes.process="*-enc *" OR Processes.process="*EncodedCommand*"
        OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*-nop*"
        OR Processes.process="*-w hidden*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-Expression*"
        OR Processes.process="*IEX(*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*"
        OR Processes.process="*Net.WebClient*")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process_name, Processes.process, Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(-enc|encodedcommand|frombase64string|-nop|-w\s+hidden|invoke-expression|iex\s*\(|downloadstring|net\.webclient)"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine

use_cases.title: Ransomware-style mass file rename / extension change
use_cases.description: Threshold detection: many files renamed in short window, often with new extension.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count, dc(Filesystem.file_name) AS files
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem
    where Filesystem.action IN ("modified","renamed")
    by Filesystem.dest, Filesystem.user, _time span=1m
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
| where files > 200
| sort - files
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where ActionType in ("FileRenamed","FileModified")
| summarize files = dcount(FileName) by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, bin(Timestamp, 1m)
| where files > 200    // empirical: > 200 unique-file renames in 1m by one account on one host
                       //            is well above the P99 of legitimate bulk-tooling
| order by files desc

use_cases.title: RMM tool installed by non-IT user — remote-access utility for hands-on-keyboard
use_cases.description: ConnectWise / AnyDesk / TeamViewer / ScreenConnect / Atera installed outside IT change windows = common tradecraft for ransomware affiliates and IT-helpdesk impersonators.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.process_name IN ("AnyDesk.exe","TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Service.exe",
        "ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ConnectWiseControl.ClientService.exe",
        "atera_agent.exe","SplashtopStreamer.exe","RustDesk.exe","NinjaOne.exe","kaseya*.exe")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process_name, Processes.process, Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("AnyDesk.exe","TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Service.exe",
        "ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ConnectWiseControl.ClientService.exe",
        "atera_agent.exe","SplashtopStreamer.exe","RustDesk.exe","NinjaOne.exe")
   or FileName matches regex @"(?i)kaseya.*\.exe"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine

use_cases.title: Scheduled task created with suspicious image / encoded args
use_cases.description: schtasks.exe /create or Microsoft-Windows-TaskScheduler EventID 4698 with LOLBin actions.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND Processes.process="*/create*"
      AND (Processes.process="*powershell*" OR Processes.process="*cmd.exe*"
        OR Processes.process="*rundll32*" OR Processes.process="*-enc*"
        OR Processes.process="*FromBase64*" OR Processes.process="*\Users\Public*"
        OR Processes.process="*\AppData\*")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process, Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/create"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("powershell","cmd.exe","rundll32","-enc","FromBase64","\Users\Public","\AppData\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName

use_cases.title: Service install for persistence — sc.exe / new service registry write
use_cases.description: Service install with binPath pointing to user-writeable path or LOLBin. Detects via process telemetry (sc.exe create) and registry (HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\* writes).
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.process_name="sc.exe" AND Processes.process="*create*"
      AND (Processes.process="*\Users\*" OR Processes.process="*\AppData\*"
        OR Processes.process="*\ProgramData\*" OR Processes.process="*\Temp\*")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process, Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| append
    [| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry
        where Registry.registry_path="*\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*"
          AND Registry.registry_value_name="ImagePath"
          AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*\Users\*"
            OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\AppData\*"
            OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\Temp\*")
        by Registry.dest, Registry.registry_path, Registry.registry_value_data, Registry.user
     | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`]
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "sc.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "create"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(\Users\|\AppData\|\ProgramData\|\Temp\)"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName

use_cases.title: Trusted vendor binary / installer launching unusual children
use_cases.description: Supply-chain trojan signal: legitimate signed binary spawning script interpreters or exotic LOLBins.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes
    where Processes.parent_process_name IN (__VENDOR_BINS__)
      AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe")
    by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.process_name, Processes.process
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
use_cases.defender_kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (__VENDOR_BINS__)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine

use_cases.title: Microsoft Teams external-tenant chat from unverified IT-helpdesk impersonator
use_cases.description: External Teams chat where displayName contains 'helpdesk' or 'IT support' — common 2024+ vishing pattern (Storm-1811, Black Basta, UNC6692). No CIM data model maps to Teams chats; uses raw O365 audit logs.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
`o365_management_activity`
  Workload=MicrosoftTeams Operation=MessageSent
  ExternalParticipants=*
| where match(SenderDisplayName, "(?i)(help.?desk|it.?support|service.?desk|tech.?support|admin)")
| stats count, earliest(_time) as firstTime, latest(_time) as lastTime
    by SenderUpn, SenderDisplayName, RecipientUpn, ChatId
use_cases.defender_kql:
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where Application == "Microsoft Teams"
| where ActionType == "MessageSent"
| where RawEventData has "ExternalParticipants"
| extend SenderDisplayName = tostring(parse_json(RawEventData).SenderDisplayName)
| where SenderDisplayName matches regex @"(?i)(help.?desk|it.?support|service.?desk|tech.?support|admin)"
| project Timestamp, AccountDisplayName, IPAddress, ActivityType, SenderDisplayName, RawEventData

use_cases.title: Asset exposure — vulnerability matches article CVE(s)
use_cases.description: Match vulnerability scanner output to the CVE(s) named in the article.
use_cases.splunk_spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    from datamodel=Vulnerabilities
    where Vulnerabilities.signature IN (__CVE_LIST__)
    by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.cve
| `drop_dm_object_name(Vulnerabilities)`
| sort - severity
use_cases.defender_kql:
// TVM tables are daily snapshots — pin a 1-day window so the engine
// partitions on Timestamp instead of scanning the full retention.
DeviceTvmSoftwareVulnerabilities
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where CveId in~ (__CVE_LIST__)
| join kind=inner (DeviceInfo | where Timestamp > ago(1d)) on DeviceId
| project DeviceName, OSPlatform, CveId, VulnerabilitySeverityLevel, RecommendedSecurityUpdate
| order by VulnerabilitySeverityLevel asc, DeviceName asc
